svg.title: AI Scientist backend — OpenAI model architecture
svg.desc: Top-down pipeline: scientist input, FastAPI, orchestrator, then Agent 1 (literature QC with gpt-4.1-mini + Tavily), a novelty gate, Agent 2 (feedback relevance with gpt-4.1-mini), Agent 3 (experiment plan with gpt-4.1 structured output), and finally storage plus response.

g.onclick: sendPrompt('What makes a good hypothesis input for the AI Scientist?')
sendPrompt('What FastAPI endpoints does this backend expose?')
sendPrompt('How does the orchestrator sequence all three agents?')
sendPrompt('How does the literature QC agent use Tavily to check novelty?')
g.text: searches scientific literature for prior work

g.onclick: sendPrompt('Which Tavily domains should we restrict to for scientific search?')
sendPrompt('What prompt does gpt-4.1-mini use to classify novelty?')
sendPrompt('What happens at the novelty gate — when do we stop vs continue?')
g.text: not found / similar / exact match → stop

g.onclick: sendPrompt('How does the feedback relevance agent decide which corrections to inject?')
g.text: finds past corrections matching this domain

g.onclick: sendPrompt('What does the feedback store schema look like?')
sendPrompt('What does the gpt-4.1 system prompt look like for experiment plan generation?')
g.text: JSON schema enforced via structured outputs

g.onclick: sendPrompt('What does the final plan JSON structure look like?')
sendPrompt('How does saving the plan enable the learning loop for future runs?')
